title: 'The war scripted by Trump is being tested by an Iranian regime determined to survive at any cost'
subhed: The resilience shown by Tehran and the spread of conflict across the Middle East have prevented the US president from declaring victory as quickly and triumphantly as he had hoped, writes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

title: 'For sale, 5-year-old child, docile and calm': Yazidi genocide trial dives into horrors of IS
subhed: The Paris criminal court heard the harrowing reality of the enslavement of a people in the name of religion. Rarely has a terrorism trial gone so far in exposing the atrocities perpetrated by jihadists.

title: Paris mayoral election: Three candidates head to second round following Knafo's withdrawal
subhed: Socialist Emmanuel Grégoire has refused any alliance with radical-left candidate Sophia Chikirou, as conservative candidate Rachida Dati looks to benefit from the withdrawal of two candidates.

title: Rosalía launches her world tour in Lyon
subhed: The Spanish pop star kicked off her Lux World Tour on Monday at the LDLC Arena, on the outskirts of Lyon, in a whirlwind of style, language and religious imagery.

title: The EU's politically sensitive debate over eurobonds
subhed: While the issue of the European Union's joint borrowing still faces strong opposition from Germany and the most fiscally orthodox European countries, it has become relatively uncontroversial among central bankers, economists and financial players.

title: French local elections: First-round results give sense of political landscape one year before presidential vote
subhed: With blurred lines between the right and far right, rivalry between the radical left and the Socialists and the fading of the presidential party, the period between the two rounds of the municipal vote is a testing ground for political power balances ahead of the 2027 election.

title: Ravens use memory, not predators, to find kills
subhed: A study conducted in Yellowstone National Park in the United States finds that corvids return to the sites of previous feasts thanks to sophisticated strategies that had completely eluded researchers until now.
